sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management
sources.population: Children, young people, and adults with suspected or diagnosed asthma, excluding severe asthma and acute attacks.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary and specialist diagnosis, monitoring, and chronic asthma management.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for chronic asthma assessment and monitoring; excludes severe asthma and acute attacks and is not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.4.6 — clinical history, examination, objective tests, and occupational asthma
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, symptom variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, atopy and family history, occupational context, respiratory examination, peak flow, FeNO, spirometry, and objective confirmation.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.6.4 — monitoring asthma control and uncontrolled asthma
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports review of symptoms, time off work or school, reliever use, systemic steroid courses, admissions or emergency attendance, validated control questionnaires, peak flow, FeNO, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking or vaping, occupational, psychosocial, seasonal and environmental factors, and response to treatment change.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.11.1 and 1.14.1–1.14.2 — inhaler technique, adherence, and action plans
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports inhaler-technique and adherence review at every review and clinician-documented personalised action-plan and trigger discussion without mapping medicine names or doses.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
sources.version: NICE NG115; updated 2019-07 with current links reviewed 2025-03
sources.population: People aged over 16 years with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary and specialist COPD diagnosis, monitoring, and ongoing management.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for adult COPD follow-up and exacerbation-feature documentation; not applicable to children and not directly validated for UAE prescribing or referral pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.137–1.2.140 and Table 6 — follow-up and review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports at least annual review of smoking, breathlessness, exercise tolerance, exacerbations, pulmonary rehabilitation, complications, medicine effects, inhaler technique, referral need, FEV1 and FVC, BMI, MRC dyspnoea, oxygen saturation, cor pulmonale, nutrition, mood, and social needs.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendation 1.3.1 — COPD exacerbation features
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician review of worsening breathlessness, cough, sputum production, and sputum colour change as exacerbation features without asserting an exacerbation diagnosis.

archive.title: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
archive.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
archive.population: People aged over 16 years with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
archive.setting: Primary and specialist COPD diagnosis, monitoring, and ongoing management.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.137–1.2.140 and Table 6 — follow-up and review
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports at least annual review of smoking, breathlessness, exercise tolerance, exacerbations, pulmonary rehabilitation, complications, medicine effects, inhaler technique, referral need, FEV1 and FVC, BMI, MRC dyspnoea, oxygen saturation, cor pulmonale, nutrition, mood, and social needs.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Recommendation 1.3.1 — COPD exacerbation features
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician review of worsening breathlessness, cough, sputum production, and sputum colour change as exacerbation features without asserting an exacerbation diagnosis.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations
sources.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE medicines, prescribing, pharmacy, discharge, and information-governance adaptation
sources.population: Adults, children, and young people using medicines, with specific transfer, reconciliation, and decision-involvement recommendations.
sources.clinical_setting: Transfers between care settings and clinician-led medicines consultations.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for documenting current medicines, changes made during transfer, reconciliation, discrepancies, person or carer involvement, values, preferences, and clinician-led decisions. It does not generate a medicine start, stop, dose, prescription, or deprescribing decision.

exact_sections.heading: Medicines-related communication between care settings
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports complete and accurate medicine information, prescribed and non-prescribed medicines, allergies, timing, monitoring, and discussion at care transitions.

exact_sections.heading: Medicines reconciliation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines reconciliation as identifying an accurate current medicine list, comparing records, recognising discrepancies, documenting changes, and including over-the-counter or complementary medicines.

exact_sections.heading: Medication review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports structured review of all medicines, views, understanding, concerns, questions, adherence context, adverse-reaction or side-effect context, monitoring, and circumstances for seeking professional advice.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.2–1.3.7 — transfer information and medicines reconciliation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting current prescribed, over-the-counter and complementary medicines, changes, discrepancies, discharge information, reconciliation and patient or carer involvement at care transitions.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1–1.6.5 — involvement, values, preferences, benefits, and harms
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting the person’s desired involvement, values, preferences, and clinician-completed discussion of treatment options, benefits and harms without generating a medicine decision.
